content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN integers - A1,A2,⋯ ,ANA_1, A_2, \cdots, A_NA1​,A2​,⋯,AN​. For each index iii find the maximum number of inversions in the entire array you can get by changing this element to any real\textbf{real}real number.

Note: The number of inversions in an array AAA is the number of pairs of indices iii, jjj such that 1≤i<j≤N1 \le i < j \le N1≤i<j≤N and Ai>AjA_i > A_jAi​>Aj​


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of 2 lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN  — the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space separated integers denoting the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, NNN space separated integers where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes the answer for the ithi^{th}ith index of the array.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.
The sum of NNN across all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1042 \cdot 10^42⋅104
There are at most 50 tests with N>10N>10N>10 and there is at most 1 test with N>50N>50N>50.
The sum of NNN across all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1032 \cdot 10^32⋅103.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Testcase 1: The initial array is [1,2][1, 2][1,2].

Let us now try to modify A1A_1A1​ to get the maximum number of inversions. We can set A1=3A_1 = 3A1​=3, and the new array [3,2][3, 2][3,2] has 1 inversion. 
We can show that this is the maximum number of inversions we can achieve by modifying A1A_1A1​, and so the first output is 111.
Let us now try to modify A2A_2A2​ to get the maximum number of inversions. We can set A2=0.5A_2 = 0.5A2​=0.5, and the new array [1,0.5][1, 0.5][1,0.5] has 1 inversion. 
We can show that this is the maximum number of inversions we can achieve by modifying A2A_2A2​, and so the second output is 111.
